sections.paragraph:
m. [but f. Psa 84:4. Root דָּרַר]-

(1) swift flight, gyration; hence concr. used of a bird wheeling in its flight; according to the Jewish interpreters the swallow (in the other member there is צִפֹּור sparrow), according to the ancient versions the turtle dove, i.q. תֹּר, which appears less suitable, Psa 84:4 Pro 26:2. Compare the root דָּרַר No. 1.

(2) a free or abundant flow (see the root No. 2). Exo 30:23, מָר־דְּרֹור myrrh which flows spontaneously. Hence-

(3) liberty, freedom (comp. נָדַב, נְדָבָה), לְ קָרָא דְרֹור to proclaim liberty to any one. Isa 61:1 Jerem. 34:8 , 15 Jer 34:15, 17 Jer 34:17followed by בְּ Lev 25:10. שְׁנַת הַדְּרֹור the year of liberation (of slaves), i.q. the year of jubilee, Eze 46:17.
